Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive light-verb idiom: noun söz + infinitive vermää; conjugates through the verb and may take a dative addressee or a clausal complement.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To promise, give one’s word, or publicly commit oneself to an action.
Usage Contexts: family agreements; workplace commitments; school duties; public speech; polite negotiation
Register and Tone: neutral; respectful; common in both everyday and formal speech
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects the high social value of a person’s word in Gagauz village, family, and community life; keeping one’s söz marks reliability.
First Example Sentence: Boba uşaklarına söz verdi: pazar günü onları Komrat panayırına götürecek.
Second Example Sentence: Mariya söz verärse, işini vakıdında bitirer.
Third Example Sentence: Toplantıda başkan söz verdi, küüyün yolu bu yılda onarılacek.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Intransitive/transitive verbal idiom with locative-directional noun araya + infinitive girmää; often appears with temporal clauses and coordinated verbs.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To intervene, mediate, or step into a disagreement so the situation can be calmed or guided.
Usage Contexts: family disputes; village mediation; workplace meetings; politics; school discipline
Register and Tone: neutral to formal; can sound conciliatory or mildly critical depending on context
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Fits communal patterns of elders, neighbors, or respected speakers calming disputes before they grow into open conflict.
First Example Sentence: İki komşu kavga edärkän, ihtär adam araya girdi da lafı tatlı bitirdi.
Second Example Sentence: Uşaklar çekişti, öğretmen araya girip hepsini dinnädi.
Third Example Sentence: Toplantıda tartışma kızışınca, Anna araya girdi da açık bir karar teklif etti.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Intransitive light-verb idiom: abstract noun maana + infinitive bulmaa; usually takes a dative or genitive target in discourse.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To look for faults, criticize small details, or make a negative remark even when the result is acceptable.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: kusur aaramak ya da gereksiz eleştirmäk
Usage Contexts: family comments; workplace reviews; humor; sarcasm; social criticism
Register and Tone: colloquial; often ironic, teasing, or mildly reproachful
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in everyday evaluative talk where public reputation, careful work, and neighborly observation carry social weight.
First Example Sentence: Ne yapsam, komşu genä maana bular, hiç razı kalmaz.
Second Example Sentence: Kızın temiz işinä maana bulmaa, çünkü o bütün gün çalıştı.
Third Example Sentence: Rapor hazırdı, ama müdür yalnız yazı tipinä maana buldu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Predicate idiom built from noun cendem + copular/light verb olmaa; subject is often an event, plan, hope, or effort.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be ruined, collapse, fail completely, or go badly after expectations were set.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: plan bozulmaa ya da iş kötü bitmää
Usage Contexts: failed plans; finances; travel; household work; sharp complaints
Register and Tone: colloquial; dramatic; emotionally strong but not necessarily vulgar
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws on Christian and folk moral imagery of hell as a place of loss, disorder, and punishment.
First Example Sentence: Yaamur başladı da bütün düün hazırlıkları cendem oldu.
Second Example Sentence: Paramız kesilinca, yolculuk planı cendem oldu.
Third Example Sentence: Bir küçük yannışlık yüzündän emeklerimiz cendem olmasın.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Transitive verb phrase: noun el + infinitive koymaa; may be literal, but idiomatically takes an object meaning property, matter, or responsibility.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To take possession of something, seize it, touch it with intent, or formally take control over a matter.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: bir şeyi kendinä almak ya da kontrol etmää
Usage Contexts: property; administration; family belongings; legal speech; workplace responsibility
Register and Tone: neutral to formal; can sound accusatory when used about taking another person’s property
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Based on the concrete gesture of the hand as a sign of control, work, ownership, and responsibility.
First Example Sentence: Kimseyin malına el koymaa, önce saabisini sor.
Second Example Sentence: Belediye eski binaya el koydu da onu müzeyä çevirdi.
Third Example Sentence: Uşak tatlıya el koydu, anası görmeyincä iki parça aldı.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Adverbial-instrumental idiom: numeral dört + instrumental noun gözlän + infinitive bakmaa; conjugates through bakmaa.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To wait eagerly, look forward to something, or expect someone with strong desire.
Usage Contexts: family visits; holidays; children’s speech; school breaks; affectionate anticipation
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses the image of doubled sight to show longing and close attention, common in family-centered rural and festive contexts.
First Example Sentence: Uşaklar dedelerini dört gözlän bakêrlar, çünkü o masal getirer.
Second Example Sentence: Biz bayram sabaanı dört gözlän baktık.
Third Example Sentence: Öğrenciler yaz kanikulalarına dört gözlän bakêr.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive-object verbal idiom: dilini as definite object + infinitive yutmaa; subject is a person who becomes silent.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To become speechless, go silent from fear, shame, surprise, or embarrassment.
Usage Contexts: school questioning; surprise; shame; family teasing; dramatic storytelling
Register and Tone: colloquial; vivid; sometimes humorous or mildly mocking
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connects speech with courage and social presence; losing the tongue means losing the ability to answer in public.
First Example Sentence: Öğretmen soru sorunca, Petri dilini yuttu, bir laf demedi.
Second Example Sentence: Haberi işidincä, bütün oda dilini yuttu.
Third Example Sentence: Toplantıda onu övdülär, o sa utancından dilini yuttu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative-transformative idiom: ablative noun peridän + object devä + infinitive yapmaa; often negated in advice.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To exaggerate a small problem and make it seem much larger than it is.
Usage Contexts: complaints; family disputes; workplace mistakes; gossip; humorous warnings
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: A Turkic-style animal comparison contrasting something tiny with a camel, a familiar image of absurd enlargement.
First Example Sentence: Bir küçük gecikmedän peridän devä yapmaa, iş hala bitmedi diil.
Second Example Sentence: Nina her ufak lafı büütüp peridän devä yapar.
Third Example Sentence: Komşular boş sözlerden peridän devä yaptılar da küüyde gürültü çıktı.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical light-verb idiom: noun anatar + infinitive bulmaa + possessive/dative complement kiminsä üreenä; complement may be replaced by a named person or group.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To find the right way to reach someone emotionally, win trust, or gain affection.
Usage Contexts: education; medicine; family reconciliation; leadership; literature; emotional persuasion
Register and Tone: poetic; warm; respectful; suitable for reflective prose
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Builds on the heart as the seat of feeling and trust, and the key as a household object that opens protected space.
First Example Sentence: Öğretmen uşakların üreenä anatar buldu, çünkü onları dinnädi.
Second Example Sentence: Komşuların üreenä anatar bulmaa için yardımsevär olmak lääzım.
Third Example Sentence: Yeni doktor hastaların üreenä anatar buldu: sakince annattı hem korkutmadı.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Imperative-prone verbal idiom: plural possessive object kulaklarını + infinitive açmaa; frequently appears as direct advice or command.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To listen carefully, pay attention, and not miss important information.
Usage Contexts: classroom instructions; elder advice; safety warnings; workplace briefings; family discipline
Register and Tone: colloquial; direct; can be caring, urgent, or mildly scolding
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects oral instruction traditions where listening to elders, teachers, and community warnings is treated as practical wisdom.
First Example Sentence: Kulaklarını aç da ihtärların nasaatını işit.
Second Example Sentence: Direktor dedi: “Kulaklarınızı açın, bu karar hepinizi ilgilendirer.”
Third Example Sentence: Yolda giderkän kulaklarını aç, traktör sesi erkenden işidiler.
